title: Cosmos Particle Burst — mic-reactive particle field that bursts on every beat
summary: A seeded field of glowing particles gently orbits the center and, on each beat (a level spike), bursts outward and brightens; each particle's size rides the spectrum at its bin and its hue comes from the Cosmos accent palette, with a central glow pulsing on overall level. It reacts to the MICROPHONE live (gracefully falls back to a synthetic signal if the mic is unavailable/denied) and ships a deterministic synthetic demo for capture. Self-contained, zero dependencies, runs fully offline.
usage_notes: RUN: open & click "enable mic" to react to your voice/music; it falls back to a built-in beat if no mic is available. KEY TECHNIQUES: a live AnalyserNode (fftSize 128) supplies a 64-bin spectrum + RMS-ish level into one render(spectrum,level,t); a beat is detected as the spike of level above a decaying envelope, which adds an outward burst impulse per particle; particle radius = base*(1+burst) plus a spectrum-driven wobble, size and brightness scale with spec[bin]; synthSpectrum(u) is a mulberry32-seeded sum of moving formant bumps plus a sharpened 4-per-loop kick so the field pulses rhythmically and loops seamlessly. CAPTURE: ?auto=1 is synthetic + window.__seek(t). TUNE: NP (particle count), RAD (field radius), burst gain, formant count/spread, kick sharpness, central glow depth.
